title: Tender Fermented Norwegian Mung Bean Sprouts Wrap
description: A chewy, fermented meal-prep dish featuring mung bean sprouts, steamed in a leaf wrap, inspired by traditional Norwegian cuisine. This recipe is satvik-friendly and packed with protein.
instructions: Rinse the mung beans and soak them in water for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the mung beans, then transfer them to a blender or food processor with 2 cups of water.
Blend the mung beans and water until they are fully broken down and the mixture is smooth.
Transfer the mixture to a large bowl and add the brown rice vinegar, coconut sugar, turmeric powder, and salt. Mix well.
Cover the bowl with a cloth and let it sit in a warm place for 24-48 hours to ferment.
After fermentation, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl. Discard the solids.
Cut the leaf wraps into large squares and place a few tablespoons of the fermented mung bean mixture on each square.
Add a sprinkle of fresh cilantro and a drizzle of avocado oil on top of the filling.
Fold the leaf wraps over the filling to enclose and steam them for 5-7 minutes or until the leaves are tender.
Serve the fermented mung bean wraps warm, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
